hypothesis_en: Mr President, ladies and gentlemen of the Conference of Presidents, ladies and gentlemen of the Bureau of Parliament, chairmen of the committees, Members of the European Parliament, ladies and gentlemen, many of those who visit Colombia often exclaim that it is a wonderful country, in stark contrast to the image of analysts and news. With the strengthening of the state, our pluralistic democracy has made the transition from formal and rhetorical guarantees to the real ones. The referendum proposed by the government and voted on 25 October was discussed and approved by Congress, modified by the opposition. Examined and reduced by the Constitutional Court, abstentions and opponents had all the space to express their theses. No one could complain of harassment of freedoms or governmental hostility. The next day, the people elected thirty governors out of one hundred and thirty-three candidates. The number of candidates doubled compared to previous contests. In several cities and departments, candidates from alternative groups to the parties won. In the campaign they led, the government's main concern was to surround them with full guarantees, and now that they are elected, the government's decision is to work with them within the Constitution, without considering the political origin. At the beginning of this government, 416 mayors were threatened by the guerrillas and 216 could not attend their meetings. Colombia has been discussing for more than a century the popular election of mayors as an essential step towards democratic enlargement, the terrorist guerrilla demanded it as a condition of peace, it was approved in the 1980s, but Here are the paradoxes of terrorism, it is the executioner who kills the local democracy he claimed. Our state is of institutions that mutually control each other and of broad participation of public opinion, which subjects citizens to the rule of law and not to the whim of the authoritarian ruler. To protect all citizens above their ideas, their economic situation, their status as entrepreneurs or workers, their affinity or disagreement with the government, confronting 30 terrorists is not an easy task. While terrorism is the great violator of human rights, the state and public forces obey it with devotion. Last year, 76 members of the institutional forces were trained in human rights. In 2002, the People's Defense received 231 complaints against the public force for abuses of international humanitarian law. The number was reduced to 160 in 2003. The control bodies, the judiciary and the government share the determination to severely sanction any violation of human rights. The value of their soldiers and police. Their sacrifice to give us peace has been immense. Since August 2003, only anti-personnel mines have killed 277 soldiers and police and 854 have been wounded, many of them permanently mutilated. We have the presence demanded by us from the Office of the United Nations High Commissioner for Human Rights. Its recommendations are received with respect. When they are not admissible to the judgment of the Government, they are discussed frankly. In almost 1 million square kilometers, with more than 400 thousand jungles, the presence of the State, supplied by terrorist organizations, has been lacking for years. In 450 municipalities we have installed soldiers and Marines called from my village who perform their military service in the locality where they reside. Between 2002 and 2003, homicides decreased by 20%, kidnappings by 26%, massacres by 33%, attacks on the population by 84%. The murder of teachers, trade unionists and journalists has decreased, and fortunately the killers of several cases are already in prison as a consequence of our determination to defeat impunity. The violent deaths of indigenous people, indigenous cooperators and environmentalists, cruelly persecuted by terrorist groups, have decreased because of the presence of the State in their ancestral territories. In the last year it has decreased by 53% and 1140 families have returned to their places of origin. Everyone who happens brings pain to the whole nation, like the car bomb that exploded in the FARC club in Nogales exactly one year ago, where 33 people were killed and 173 were injured, or the peasant of the Union Penella, who was burned alive by the terrorist guerrillas because he refused to leave his home and go to enlarge the number of displaced, or the peasant woman from Tibu who lost her leg The constitutional reform introducing anti-terrorist standards was approved by Congress in the midst of national and international debate. The powers granted to the public force to capture, intercept and intercept in cases of terrorism were subject to the subsequent approval of a statutory law. I think the world should review the concept of presuming that this type of powers given to governments puts the people at risk. I call violent groups terrorists because of the nature of their actions, the democratic context of Colombia, the international legislation, the source of their resources and the result of their activity. He places anti-personal mines, kills children, indulges in his own cruelty, acts with lewdness and vice, can only be called a terrorist, who acts against democracy through violent action, in the midst of the Colombian determination to guarantee all expressions of political thought, can only be called a terrorist. A terrorist who, for ideological or various reasons, acts or threatens with violence is defined by the legislation of many countries as a terrorist, just as a terrorist who acts in Colombia can only be called a terrorist who finances violence with illicit drugs and kidnappings, with the destruction of the dignity of the people, their freedom and their I am not opposed to the negotiated solution, but without mixing it with terrorism, with the cessation of hostilities as a requirement, however, that the Colombia has had a generous tradition of negotiating solutions with violent groups. I have given priority to democratic security because I believe that it will make the violent understand the need to negotiate seriously, so as to receive the total defeat by the institutions. In the intervention of the Group of Friends and the Facilitating Commission, we have sought dialogue with the ELN, so far without results, despite our provision, they fear to take any step towards peace without the permission of the FARC. It is time to perfect and monitor the cessation of hostilities, for which the support of the Organization of American States is of the greatest use, which must lead to a rapid concentration of the members of these groups who have a sincere will for peace. We demand the restoration of the principle of exclusive institutional protection of citizens. To this end, the demobilization of self-defense contributes. The military action of the government has been relentless. The number of casualties increased by 85 in 2003 compared to 2002 and the number of prisoners increased by 135 in the same period. It seeks to facilitate peace agreements with impunity, in harmony with justice, although its procedure coincides with the process that is advanced with self-defence, it is needed most for the guerrilla, which is superior in members and registers the largest number of disbanded in the course of the current government. The pardons of the past for heinous crimes today are unacceptable and it would also be unacceptable if tomorrow the law we approve were amended in accordance with the political predisposition towards other terror actors who want to negotiate. The FARC has more than a thousand kidnapped in its power, including congressmen, members of the public force and the former presidential candidate Ingrid Betancourt, who is I understand the pain of their families and the international interest in a humanitarian agreement. The basis of a negotiation through the United Nations, the release of all those kidnapped and the commitment of terrorist guerrillas who leave prisons not to commit crimes again, with effective guarantees, such as their residence in a country that protects the agreement. Otherwise, it would be to mistreat the sacrifice of public power, to discourage its action and to wither the popular spirit of respect for the law, it would be to open the door to a recurrent extortion of the state and society. In war there are contenders who are recognized as legitimate for the effect of humanitarian agreements, but in terrorism there is a executioner and a victim, which is society, whose rights and virtues we must protect with the force of institutional weapons. The murder of Gilberto Echeverry, Guillermo Gaviria, Doris Hill, Helmut Bickembach, Chicao, Muramatsu and a large number of members of the public force, murder of defenseless citizens committed by Lafarge when he claimed the presence of the authority in the vicinity of Colombia is a social democracy, 63% of the nation's budget, excluding debt services, is allocated to social protection, education and health directly and through decentralization. The government is working on a plan called the Seven Tools of Equity, which are the educational revolution, social protection, the boost to the solidarity economy, the social management of the countryside, the social management of public services, the country of owners and the quality of urban life, the coverage of basic education. The State Technical Education Service will achieve its goal of moving from 1 million students per year to 4 million in the quarter. The different child nutrition programmes favour 5 million children. The credit for small businesses grew last year by 57%. We have a lot of social problems, but we are accompanied by the whole decision to overcome them. The economy grew last year between 3.7 and 4 when it was expected to be 2 in the last year, unemployment rates fell by 3 and that scourge of unemployment was located at 12.3 In Colombia, inequality is not due to private enterprise, but to the lack of private enterprise. The organized private enterprise has high burdens to contribute to solidarity with its workers and the rest of the population. Our employers pay most of the tax obligations and the latest tax reforms have increased their taxes. We are enthusiastically promoting workers' companies, cooperatives and the participation of users and workers in the provision of public services. The European Union has given us very valuable support, which I want to thank you for. The London Declaration deeply engages us. It has been a great source of democratic commitment. A great European support framework, the financing of peace laboratories is welcomed by the Community, we need more cooperation to defeat drugs, without drugs there would be no terrorism, it is not time to blame some for producing it and others for consuming it. Our farmers who grow it feel deceived by terrorists and affected in their dignity. They long for alternatives. We already have 20 families of forest rangers to take care of the coca-free areas and supervise the recovery of the jungle. We need to reach 50 families. Young Europeans need to know that the drug has destroyed 1.7 million hectares of tropical rainforest in Colombia, precisely in access to the Amazon basin, seriously threatened by this scourge. We thank the extension of the general system of preferences and respectfully request that the Council of Ministers of the European Union, the European Parliament and the Council of Ministers of the European Union, in their joint declaration, I come to this Parliament with deep respect, with gratitude for the interest paid and the permanent aid to Colombia. I am aware that the solutions that my government is looking for for the complex problems of my country are controversial. It builds more deliberation than absence, it builds more debate than withdrawal, it builds more constructive controversy than abandonment of the curules. My generation has not lived a day of peace, the homeland we want for our children and those who will come must be a homeland of peace and equity.
reference_en: President (1.05) (3.14) Members of the Conference of Presidents Members of the Bureau of the European Parliament Chairmen of Committees Members of the European Parliament ladies and gentlemen (2.21) among those who visit Colombia many say that it is a wonderful country in a blatant contrast with the image given by analysts and news others say that it is an illegitimate authoritarian and elitist state with the strengthening of the state our pluralist democracy (1.02) has moved on from formal rhetoric guarantees to real effective guarantees the referendum proposed by the government and voted through on the twenty-fifth of October was discussed and adopted by the Congress the text was modified by the opposition and examined and reduced by the Constitutional Court abstentionists and opponents had all the margin of manoeuvre they needed to express their own views nobody can complain about a restriction of freedoms or about governmental hostility the following day the people elected thirty governors among one hundred and thirty-three candidates nine hundred and fourteen mayors from three thousand four hundred and forty-one candidates three hundred and ninety-eight Members of Parliament from three hundred and seventy-three lists made up of two thousand seven hundred and twenty-three candidates a= and the number of candidates was doubled in comparison with previous figures in many cities and departments candidates from groups which were alternative to the traditional parties won they came from organisations which had emerged from former guerrillas also called left-wing or opposition groups in the countryside which they represented the fundamental concern of the government was to provide them with full guarantees and now that they are elected the government has decided to work with them within the Constitution without taking account of the political origin of their elections but to work with transparency and above all with a patriotic desire to unite Colombia within its diversity mayors and governors who were elected by the people have been subject to pressure and terrorist attacks at the beginning of this government four hundred and sixteen mayors were threatened by the ~guerrillia and two hundred and sixteen could not carry out their responsibilities in the area for which they were responsible thanks to work done on security (1.29) the problem only continues in fourteen different places for more than a century Colombia discussed p= the people's election of mayors as an essential step towards extending democracy the guerrilla (1.63) wanted this as a condition of peace and it was adopted in the nineteen eighties but the paradox of terrorism means that it is the henchman who assassinate= assassinates the local democracy which they called for in the first place our state is one of institutions which control one and oth= another and there is broad involvement of the general public and the citizens are subject to the law and not to the caprices or whims of an authoritarian government the concept of security of the present government is democratic we want to protect all citizens beyond their ideas their economic situation their situation as entrepreneurs or workers and whether they agree with the government or not (1.04) facing up to thirty thousand terrorists is not an easy task we are aware that we have to face the challenge to overcome terrorism with transparency as a prerequisite to build national unity and to give sustainability to democratic security a= and we want to restore law and order (1.36) while terrorism is the violator of human rights the state and the public forces of order are pursuing them with ~persistent last year seventy-six thousand eight hundred and sixty-four people from the forces of the institutions were trained in human rights in the year two thousand and two the Public Prosecutor received two hundred and thirty-one charges against the public forces of order for abusing humanitarian international law their number was reduced to one hundred and sixty in two thousand and three the bodies of control the justice and the government share a determination to sanction severely any proved violation of human rights (1.18) Colombia is grateful to and pays tribute to the courage of its soldiers and policemen their sacrifice to give us peace has been immense since August two thousand and three simply because of anti-personnel mines two hundred and seventy-seven soldiers and eight hun= have been killed and eight hundred and fifty-four have been injured many of them being crippled for life (1.84) our country is open to the vigilance of the international community we have the High Commissioner of Human Rights of the United Nations in our country this Office's recommendations are received with respect when their recommendations cannot be carried out with= in the view of the government they are discussed openly I recognise the role played by human rights NGOs I appreciate the task of many of them although I have to express my disagreement with reports which do not correspond to reality with one million two hundred thousand square kilometres and more than four hundred and thousand square kilometres of jungle ~the_ state has not been present for many years in some of these areas and that role has been fulfilled by the terrorist organisations we want to extend institutional protection to our citizens (3.41) now the police is present in all areas although we shall not cease in our efforts until the last of these urban areas is protected in four hundred and fifty towns we have moved in soldiers and navy infrant= infantry under the heading My Village and they carry out their military service in the places where they live there have been very good results and we hope to continue to count on their (1.22) their help (2.36) as some people who will always respect the law and give leadership between two thousand and two thousand and three assassinations were reduced by twenty per cent kidnappings by twenty-six per cent massacres by thirty-three per cent attacks on the people by eighty-four per cent in January two thousand and four (2.18) there were fifty-one kidnappings as compared with one hundred eighty-six in January two thousand and three and murders were reduced (2.35) considerably killings of teachers trade union representatives and journalists ~has gone down and (1.4) the perpetrators are in prison as a consequence of our determination to overcome impunity we wish to give special protection to teachers trade union representatives and journalists because no democracy can allow these people to be killed violent deaths of native peoples or native peoples working in cooperation or en= or environmental groups who have been persecuted cruelly by terrorist groups has gone down considerably because the state is now present in these ancestral lands internal displacement is one of our great tragedies as a= which has resulted from terrorism last year (2.66) this went down by fifty-three percent and eleven thousand one hundred and forty families returned to their places of origin although we have made ~prograss progress we still have a great deal of work to do what country can be happy with two thousand two hundred kidnappings a year we can only cry victory when the massacres and acts of terror are totally eradicated because any which happens brings grief to the whole country like the booby-trapped car which was set off by FARC in the club El Nogal and which caused the deaths of thirty-three persons or (1.2) the farmer who was burnt alive by guerrillas because he refused to abandon his house and join the numbers of those displaced or the peasant woman in ~Tibool who lost her leg because a bomb went off when she was walking along the track with her five old daughter the constitutional reform which we are introducing will provide anti-terrorist laws and this was adopted by the Congress in the middle of the or by means of national debate the responsibilities given to the public forces of order to capture (1.21) terrorists (2.2) are dependent on the adoption of another statutory law and we want to give guarantees of the Public Prosecutor being informed within a period of thirty-six hours (1.98) I know that this legislation is less strong than that exists in many Western democracies I think that the world should change its mind about presuming that the type of options given to the governments undermines human rights because human rights require identical full surveillance both by the justice and by the executive body (1.21) groups which are violent in nature because of the actions they carry out are terrorists we have a democratic context in Colombia we have international comparative legislation (1.35) those who place anti-personnel mines kill children and overlook their own cruelty (1.1) act in an underhand stealthy way can only be described as terrorists anybody who acts against democracy by means of violent action (1.82) against the Colombian determination to give guarantees to all expressions of political thinking can only be described as terrorists anyone who for ideological or other reasons (1.67) acts or threatens violence (1.51) would be defined as a terrorist by the legislation of many countries and in the same way these people who act like this in Colombia can only be described as terrorists (1.4) anybody who finances violence by means of illegal drugs and kidnappings destroying the dignity of the people in the village their freedom their ecology can only be described as terrorists anybody who uses poverty and inequality as an excuse to justify violence and actually causes more poverty more inequality by carrying out violence those people can only be described as terrorists I am not opposed to a negotiated solution but without confusing it with terrorism we must have a cease of hostilities (2.78) although disarmament and demobilisation requires a long time Colombia has had a generous tradition of negotiating solutions with violent groups my predecessor President Andrés Pastrana made an immense effort to negotiate with FARC the good faith and patriotism of that representative were trampled on by FARC in a sho= in a cowardly way which increased their criminal activity when the country was hoping for peace I've given priority to democratic security because I believe that (1.69) it will make the violent people understand the need to negotiate in a serious way nonetheless (1.1) considerable efforts have been made to initiate negotiations with those who are violent with the help of the Catholic Church of Cuba with the help of the Group of Friendly Countries and the Facilitating Commission we have sought dialogue with the ELN so far there have been no results despite our willingness (1.98) they fear to take any step towards peace without the permission of FARC (1.92) there has been a process of medie= mediation through the Catholic Church with vigilantes and I've decided to continue that I agreed that the Peace Commission should initiate dialogue once these organisations announce the cease of hostilities it is now time to see to it that hostilities are brought to an end (2.18) and the support given by the Organisation of American States has been the greatest help because in this way we can have a concentration on the groups which really want peace (2.21) (1.58) when a= the state and society are attacked this is an attack on legal order but these people pretend that they're defending society although they have recourse to violence and they use groups to carry out private justice we want to return to the principle of institutional protection for the citizens and the demobilisation of the vigilantes will help that the government has not ceased its efforts against these vigilante groups which have not respected the cease of hostilities the number of those who have ceased this activity has gone up by eight-five per cent in two thousand and three in comparison with two thousand and two and the number of those captured increased by one hundred and thirty-five per cent during the same period the draft law which is being discussed at great length seeks to facilitate peace agreements without impunity and in harmony with justice (1.1) although its development is happening at the same time as the action on the vigilante groups it is needed to fight against the guerrilla which has more members and in which there has been the largest number of demobilisation in the course of the present go= government (1.31) with this law we have to look at the past and look ahead to the future the pardons given in the past for atrocious crimes are unacceptable and will be so tomorrow when the law is amended (3.21) in the period of this government (1.3) almost four thousand five hundred members of violent groups have been demobilised sixty-eight per cent of them are members of the guerrilla the FARC they have been received generously and the state is making huge efforts to help them to be rehabilitated and to be brought back into society (1.33) this number exceeds that achieved in the peace process in the last few decades the FARC has more than one thousand people kidnapped in its power among those members of Congress members of the public forces of order and the former presidential ca= candidate Ingrid Betancourt a Colombian and French citizen I understand the grief of their families and the international interest in a humanitarian agreement initially I made this dependent on the cease of hostilities and the initiation of dialogue my conversations with the Church the French government the secretariat o= of the UN Kofi Annan and the family members of victims led me to accept (1.16) the idea of seeking an agreement on the basis of a ne= negotiations carried out through the United Nations so that we can have freedom for all those kidnapped and a promise from the guerrillas who leave prison that they will not commit crimes again with actual guarantees (2.53) without a real guarantee that they will not commit crimes again the government cannot free guerrillas or terrorists this would be disdaining the sacrifice of the public forces of order it would be discouraging them and discouraging people's desire to respect the law it would be opening the door for (1.01) s= for extortion of the state and society (1.04) we have to bear in mind that (1.26) citizens are kidnapped (2.52) and terrorists are in prison war is one thing terrorism is something different in war there are circumstances which are recognised as being legitimate because we have humanitarian agreements but in terrorism there is one henchman and one victim which is society the rights and virtues of which we have to protect with the force of institutional weapons (1.05) and this is why the authorities cannot give in to blackmail (1.59) despite the cruelty of the henchman proved in the assassination of Gilberto Echeverri Guillermo ~Conea Doris Gil Helmut Bickenback Chikao Muramatsu and a large number of members of the public forces of order (2.2) killing innocent citizens as carried out by FARC (3.73) cannot be justified no negotiation can lead to a strengthening of the henchman Colombia is a social democracy sixty-three per cent of the country's budget excluding rescheduling the debt is used for social protection education and health directly and through decentralisation terrorism has exacerbated poverty (1.34) creating peace requires means that we have to overcome this poverty the government is working on a plan known as the seven instruments of equity which are a revolution in education social welfare (1.5) encouraging people to be part of the real economy rural development developing the public services and improving the quality of life in the cities the provision of basic ~edu_cation has reached eight= eighty-six point three per cent the education service will achieve its challenge in the next few years to move from one million seventy thousand students a year to four million a year the different programmes for child nutrition are reaching five million children and loans for small companies have increased by fifty per c= seven per cent in the last year we have many social problems but we are all determined to overcome these the economy grew last year from three point seven to four per cent when we were expecting a two-percent increase last year the unemployment levels went down by three per cent and reached twelve point three per cent in Colombia (1.11) inequality is not due to private entrepreneurship but a lack of private entrepreneurship private entrepreneurship (1.53) can contribute to solidarity with workers and the rest of the people our employers are (2.28) doing everything they can to (1.58) make an effort along these lines terrorism has a= slowed down the expansion of the private economy and this has increased people moving onto the grey market we are encouraging companies made up by workers cooperatives or the full involvement of workers and users in the provision of public services we believe in social compou= capitalism to fight against the areas where drug trafficking is concentrated we have adopted a law to try to cut back on their domination and in the last year we have moved from five sentences to sixty-one the European Union has provided us with very valuable help and support and I wish to thank you for that the London Declaration is one which moved us deeply (1.04) it was a source of democratic commitment (1.68) a great framework for European support the financing of the peace la= laboratories is something which is very welcome in our community we need more cooperation to overcome drugs without drugs we would not have terrorism (1.83) I don't want to accuse those who produce these drugs or others who consume them but the problem is so great that any permissive country ends up being a producer a trafficker and a consumer it is now time to defeat drugs our farmers who cultivate it feel fooled led astray by terrorists and their dignity has been impaired they are longing for alternatives we already have twenty thousand families acting as forest keepers to look after the areas which are free of coca and to look after the recovery of the jungle they receive about two thousand dollars a year and we need to extend this to fifty thousand families young Europeans must know that drugs have destroyed one million seven hundred thousand hectares of tropical jungle in Colombia (2.49) particularly on the edge of the Amazon basin which is already threatened by this scourge in the Andean Community we are concerned that the European Union did not make a decision to negotiate a free trade treaty with our countries when it did so with Mexico and Chile and it is intending to do so with Mercosur the defects of Andean integration which are put forward as an excuse not to negotiate this treaty must be overcome by the obligations of the agreement with the European Union we are very grateful about the extension of the generalised preferences system and we would like it to be maintained until replaced by a free p= trade agreement of a permanent nature I come to this Parliament with the deepest respect with gratitude for the interest shown and faith in creative thinking (2.3) (1.83) we must have debate rather than destruction we must have constructive controversy that's better than anything else (4.8) (1.51) you know from= about my intransigence in the face of terrorism my generation has not lived a day of peace the country which we want to create for our children (1.73) should be a country of peace and fairness I should like to ask you (2.49) ho= Honourable Members of Parliament to understand my profound love for democracy thank you
source_es: Señor Presidente (1.57) señores miembros de la conferencia de Presidentes señores miembros de la mesa del Parlamento Presidentes de las comisiones eurodiputados señoras y señores (2.14) muchos entre quienes visitan a Colombia suelen exclamar que es un país maravilloso en franco contraste con la imagen de analistas y noticias (1.27) otros expresan que es un Estado ilegítimo autoritario y elitista (1.99) con el fortalecimiento del Estado nuestra democracia pluralista ha hecho el tránsito de las garantías formales y retóricas a las efectivas y reales el referendo propuesto por el gobierno y votado el veinticinco de octubre (1.06) fue discutido y aprobado por el Congreso modificado su texto por la oposición (1.01) examinado y reducido por la Corte Constitucional abstencionistas y opositores dispusieron de todos los espacios para la expresión de sus tesis nadie pudo quejarse de cercenamiento de libertades o de hostilidad gubernamental al día siguiente el pueblo eligió treinta gobernadores entre ciento treinta y tres candidatos (1.06) novecientos catorce alcaldes entre tres mil cuatrocientos cuarenta y un candidatos (1.14) trescientos noventa y ocho diputados entre trescientas setenta y tres listas integradas por dos mil setecientos veintitrés candidatos (1.09) el número de candidatos se duplicó en relación con certámenes anteriores (1.65) en varias ciudades y departamentos (1.08) triunfaron candidatos de grupos alternativos a los partidos tradicionales (1.25) de organizaciones derivadas de antiguas guerrillas de agrupaciones llámense de izquierda o de oposición (1.07) en la campaña que ellos adelantaron la preocupación fundamental del gobierno fue rodearlo de plenitud de garantías y ahora que están elegidos la decisión del gobierno es trabajar con ellos dentro de la constitución sin considerar el origen político s= de su elección con transparencia y por sobre todo con el designio patriótico de unir a Colombia dentro de la diversidad alcaldes y gobernadores de elección popular han sido víctimas de presiones y asesinatos de terroristas (1.04) al inicio de este gobierno cuatrocientos dieciséis alcaldes estaban amenazados por la guerrilla y doscientos dieciséis no podían atender sus responsabilidades en el territorio de su jurisdicción (1.18) la tarea adelantada en seguridad ha permitido que solamente en catorce localidades subsista el problema Colombia discutió durante más de un siglo la elección popular de alcaldes como paso esencial de ampliación democrática la guerrilla terrorista la demandaba como condición de paz (1.04) fue aprobada en los años ochenta pero es aquí la paradoja del terrorismo es el verdugo que asesina a la democracia local que re= que reclamó (1.38) nuestro Estado es de instituciones que mutuamente se controlan y de amplia participación de opinión pública lo cual somete a los ciudadanos al imperio de la ley y no al capricho del gobernante autoritario (1.39) el concepto de seguridad del gobierno que presido es democrático para proteger a todos los ciudadanos por encima de sus ideas su situación económica su condición de empresarios o trabajadores su afinidad o desacuerdo con el gobierno (1.25) enfrentar treinta mil terroristas no es tarea fácil (1.35) somos conscientes que tenemos el reto de superar el desafío terrorista con transparencia como presupuesto para construir la unidad nacional y dar sostenibilidad a la seguridad democrática que busca restaurar la ley y el orden (1.35) mientras el terrorismo es el gran violador de derechos humanos el Estado y la fuerza pública los acatan con devoción (1.17) el año pasado fueron capacitados en derechos humanos setenta y seis mil ochocientos sesenta y cuatro integrantes de las fuerzas institucionales (1.06) en el año dos mil dos la Defensoría del Pueblo recibió doscientas treinta y una denuncias contra la fuerza pública por abuso de derecho internacional humanitario el número se redujo a ciento sesenta en dos mil tres los órganos de control la justicia y el gobierno compartimos la determinación de sancionar con severidad cualquier violación comprobada a los derechos humanos (1.41) Colombia agradece y exalta el valor de sus soldados y policías (1.04) su sacrificio para darnos tranquilidad ha sido inmenso desde agosto de dos mil tres solamente por minas antipersonales han sido asesinados doscientos setenta y siete soldados y policías y ochocientos cincuenta y cuatro han resultado heridos muchos de ellos definitivamente mutilados nuestro país está abierto a la vigilancia de la comunidad internacional (1.08) contamos con la presencia reclamada por nosotros de la Oficina del Alto comisionado de derechos humanos de Naciones Unidas sus recomendaciones se reciben con respeto cuando no son atendibles a juicio del gobierno se discute con franqueza (1.17) reconozco el espacio de las organizaciones no gubernamentales de derechos humanos valoro la tarea de muchas sin perjuicio de expresar mis desacuerdos en relación a informes que no correspondan a los hechos (1.32) en casi millón doscientos mil kilómetros cuadrados con más de cuatrocientos mil de selva ha faltado por años la presencia del Estado suplida por las organizaciones terroristas nos hemos propuesto ampliar la capacidad institucional de protección a la ciudadanía (1.13) de los mil cien municipios en ciento setenta no había presencia de policía hoy está en todos aunque no descansaremos hasta cuando el último de los cascos urbanos esté protegido en cuatrocientos cincuenta co= municipios hemos instalado califico de terroristas a los grupos violentos por la naturaleza de sus acciones el contexto democrático de Colombia la legislación internacional comparada la fuente de sus recursos y el resultado de su actividad a quien coloca minas antipersonales asesina niños se solaza de su propia crueldad procede con alevosía y sevicia solamente se puede denominar terrorista a quien actúa en contra de la democracia a través de la acción violenta en medio de la determinación colombiana de dar garantías a todas las expresiones del pensamiento político solamente se puede denominar terrorista a quien por razones ideológicas o de diversa índole proceda o amenace con violencia la legislación de muchos países lo define como terrorista de igual modo a quien así procede en Colombia solamente se puede denominar terrorista a quien financia la violencia con drogas ilícitas y secuestros con destrucción de la dignidad del pueblo de su libertad y su ecología solamente se puede denominar terrorista a quien invoca la pobreza y la desigualdad para justificar la violencia y provoca más pobreza y más desigualdad con el ejercicio de la violencia solamente se puede denominar terrorista no me opongo a la
